title: Vale
content:
Good-bye, sweet friend, good-bye,
     And all the world must be
     Between my friend and me;
     And nothing is, dear heart,
     But hands that meet to part;
     Good-bye, sweet friend, good-bye.

     Good-bye, sweet love, good-bye,
     And one long grave must be
     Between my love and me;
     What comfort there, dear heart,
     For hands that meet to part?
     Good-bye, sweet love, good-bye.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Dora Sigerson Shorter, titled "Vale", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
